title: Technology, Culture, and the Next AI Interface with signüll
content: Erik Torenberg and Anish Acharya from a16z discuss how technology reshapes culture, relationships, and product design. They explore the difference between tacit and intellectual knowledge, the impact of dating apps on human connection, AI-driven relationships, and why Claude feels artisanal compared to more utilitarian models. The conversation offers insights for consumer founders on what truly matters in developing technology that aligns with cultural shifts.

title: 中国政府为何对人工智能感到担忧
content: China’s government is uneasy about artificial intelligence because it can rapidly alter the economy, amplify social inequality, and shift power dynamics. AI’s ability to automate jobs threatens employment, while its influence over information flows risks political control and social division. Concerns also focus on data privacy, cybersecurity, and the potential for AI to be used unethically. These factors make the authorities cautious about unchecked AI development and deployment.

title: 那个被时代抛弃的诺基亚，杀回来了
content: Nokia’s stock has risen to a 16‑year high as the former mobile‑phone titan pivots to AI infrastructure. After selling its handset business in 2013, Nokia focused on telecom equipment and, through acquisitions of Alcatel-Lucent and Infinera, became a leader in optical networking—critical for AI data centers. The company’s 2025 market share in global fibre networks reached 21%, and a $1 billion NVidia investment spurred its share price climb. The result is a reinvigorated, AI‑focused Nokia.

title: 世界如何为石油短缺做准备
content: The article explains how nations and industries are responding to the threat of future oil shortages. It examines measures such as boosting alternative energy production, improving fuel efficiency, diversifying supply chains, investing in petrochemical substitutes, and encouraging public transport. Countries are also adjusting policies, including stockpiling strategies and regional cooperation, to mitigate disruptions. The piece highlights both short‑term tactics and long‑term structural changes aimed at reducing global reliance on fossil fuels.

title: 找到了 ai coding 的最性价比解决方案， GPT/Claude 模型畅用
content: The author compares past AI coding solutions—domestic models that perform poorly, expensive GPT/Claude plans, and unreliable intermediaries—and finds a new approach that offers cost‑effective access to GPT/Claude official memberships. Customers can use their own API credits (5.3 codex, 5.4, Claude Sonnet/Opus) in any software while monitoring real‑time quota usage. After a few days of use, the high‑intensity coding estimates about $150/month. Detailed implementation is not disclosed.

title: 2026 04 16 HackerNews
content: The Hacker News round‑up covers privacy‑risks of Flock Safety’s AI surveillance, legal disputes over data deletion under CCPA, and criticism of the company’s monitoring model. It details Flock’s rapid expansion, lawsuit against Anna’s Archive and Spotify, and broader issues of unchecked mass surveillance. Other stories discuss compiler learning resources, debates on open‑source versus closed‑source security, and the cost of quality decline in VF’s backpack brands, highlighting trade‑offs between business strategy and consumer protection.

title: 大卫·扎斯拉夫与激励机制的专制
content: David Zaslav’s leadership at streaming giants illustrates how incentive structures can create an over‑emphasis on rapid growth, sometimes at the expense of quality, talent, and long‑term sustainability. By prioritizing subscriber numbers and quarterly gains, Zaslav’s management style encourages risk‑taking, short‑term thinking, and high‑pressure work environments. The article argues that such a powerful reward system may drive a culture akin to tyranny, stifling dissent and leading to unchecked corporate ambition.
